prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

```pddl
(submit_payment_instruction originating_agent_instance)
(allocate_settlement_route_to_instruction originating_agent_instance primary_settlement_route_instance)
(assign_screening_service_to_instruction originating_agent_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)
(approve_payment_instruction_for_processing originating_agent_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)
(reserve_origin_channel_for_instruction originating_agent_instance origin_channel_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)

(release_funding_source_from_instruction originating_agent_instance funding_source_instance)

(release_funding_source_from_instruction beneficiary_agent_instance funding_source_instance)

(approve_payment_instruction_for_processing settlement_processor_primary_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)
(stage_settlement_file settlement_processor_primary_instance settlement_file_primary_instance settlement_message_instance)

(progress_reconciliation_without_destination_exception settlement_processor_primary_instance configuration_parameter_instance funding_source_instance settlement_file_primary_instance settlement_message_instance)

(advance_exception_review_without_flagged_exceptions settlement_processor_primary_instance escalation_policy_instance settlement_file_primary_instance settlement_message_instance)
(close_processor_work_item settlement_processor_primary_instance)
(release_screening_service_from_instruction settlement_processor_primary_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)

(release_escalation_policy_from_processor settlement_processor_primary_instance escalation_policy_instance)
(release_funding_source_from_instruction settlement_processor_primary_instance funding_source_instance)
(assign_screening_service_to_instruction settlement_processor_primary_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)
(attach_duplicate_check_record_to_instruction settlement_processor_primary_instance duplicate_check_record_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)
(complete_processor_settlement settlement_processor_primary_instance fallback_settlement_route_instance duplicate_check_record_instance)
(release_screening_service_from_instruction settlement_processor_primary_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)
(submit_payment_instruction settlement_processor_secondary_instance)
(allocate_settlement_route_to_instruction settlement_processor_secondary_instance fallback_settlement_route_instance)
(assign_screening_service_to_instruction settlement_processor_secondary_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)
(approve_payment_instruction_for_processing settlement_processor_secondary_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)
(stage_settlement_file settlement_processor_secondary_instance settlement_file_secondary_instance settlement_message_instance)
(validate_settlement_file settlement_processor_secondary_instance settlement_file_secondary_instance settlement_message_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)
(assign_configuration_parameter_to_processor settlement_processor_secondary_instance configuration_parameter_instance)
(bind_funding_source_to_instruction settlement_processor_secondary_instance funding_source_instance)
(progress_reconciliation_without_destination_exception settlement_processor_secondary_instance configuration_parameter_instance funding_source_instance settlement_file_secondary_instance settlement_message_instance)
(assign_escalation_policy_to_processor settlement_processor_secondary_instance escalation_policy_instance)
(advance_exception_review_without_flagged_exceptions settlement_processor_secondary_instance escalation_policy_instance settlement_file_secondary_instance settlement_message_instance)
(close_processor_work_item settlement_processor_secondary_instance)
(release_screening_service_from_instruction settlement_processor_secondary_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)
(release_configuration_parameter_from_processor settlement_processor_secondary_instance configuration_parameter_instance)
(release_escalation_policy_from_processor settlement_processor_secondary_instance escalation_policy_instance)
(release_funding_source_from_instruction settlement_processor_secondary_instance funding_source_instance)
(assign_screening_service_to_instruction settlement_processor_secondary_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)
(attach_duplicate_check_record_to_instruction settlement_processor_secondary_instance duplicate_check_record_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)
(complete_processor_settlement settlement_processor_secondary_instance fallback_settlement_route_instance duplicate_check_record_instance)
(release_screening_service_from_instruction settlement_processor_secondary_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)
(assign_screening_service_to_instruction originating_agent_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)
(attach_duplicate_check_record_to_instruction originating_agent_instance duplicate_check_record_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)
(complete_originating_settlement originating_agent_instance primary_settlement_route_instance duplicate_check_record_instance)
(release_screening_service_from_instruction originating_agent_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)
(assign_screening_service_to_instruction beneficiary_agent_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)
(attach_duplicate_check_record_to_instruction beneficiary_agent_instance duplicate_check_record_instance compliance_screening_service_instance)
(complete_beneficiary_settlement beneficiary_agent_instance secondary_settlement_route_instance duplicate_check_record_instance)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is complex, involving many objects and actions for finance payments settlement with duplicate payment prevention. I need to find a plan that achieves the goal: settlement_completed for originating_agent_instance, beneficiary_agent_instance, settlement_processor_primary_instance, and settlement_processor_secondary_instance.


## Understanding the Problem  
- The task involves managing finance payment settlements with multiple agents and processors.  
- The goal is to ensure settlement completion for originating agent, beneficiary agent, primary processor, and secondary processor.  
- Duplicate payment prevention is a key requirement to avoid errors in the settlement process.

## Examining Type Relationships  
- Originating agent, beneficiary agent, and settlement processors are subtypes of payment_instruction, aligning with the settlement_completed predicate.  
- This means all can be treated as payment_instructions for settlement actions, simplifying the process.  

## Identifying Settlement Actions  
- Actions like complete_originating_settlement and complete_processor_settlement set settlement_completed, requiring conditions like duplicate_control_applied.  
- Each action releases bound resources, such as routing options and duplicate check records, after completion.  

## Checking Dependencies  
- Duplicate_control_applied requires entity_settlement_released, set by actions like release_originating_agent or close_processor_work_item.  
- Screening service compliance_screening and one duplicate_check_record_instance must be shared across all settlements, suggesting sequencing is needed.

## Modeling the Process  
- The problem involves settling payments for originating agent (O), beneficiary agent (B), primary processor (P1), and secondary processor (P2) without duplication, using shared resources like routes and screening services.  
- Each must follow steps: submit, allocate a route, assign screening, and approve, but with only one compliance_screening_service, sequencing is necessary to avoid conflicts.  

## Sequencing Shared Resources  
- Screening service must be assigned to one instruction at a time, requiring release after approval via release_screening_service_from_instruction.  
- Routes and funding sources are limited, suggesting a need to complete and release resources for one before starting another to avoid bottlenecks.  

## Handling Processor Readiness  
- Processors need ready_for_processing, achieved by approve_payment_instruction_for_processing, which requires submitted instruction, selected route, and assigned screening.  
- Actions like assign_configuration_parameter_to_processor assume this readiness, applicable since processors are subtypes of payment_instruction.  

## Exploring Release Paths  
- Entity_settlement_released, needed for duplicate control, is set for processors via closing actions like validate and progress recon, assuming no exceptions.  
- For agents, release_originating_agent requires conditions like origin_context_locked and message_composed, indicating additional setup steps.

## Planning Agent Sequencing  
- Agents (O and B) require channels ready or on hold to compose messages, starting with no channels ready initially.  
- Reserve_channel action needs ready_for_processing agent, screening_assigned, and channel_assigned, setting channels to ready status.  

## Managing Shared Resources  
- Screening and funding sources are shared, requiring sequencing: complete and release resources for one agent before starting the next.  
- For O, after submitting and allocating a route, assign screening, approve for processing, then reserve and commit origin channel with funding.  

## Handling Beneficiary Steps  
- For B, similar steps apply, but use a destination channel and a different route, such as secondary_settlement_route_instance.  
- After channels are ready, both agents need origin_channel_ready, destination_channel_ready, and contexts locked to compose the message.  

## Ensuring Screening Continuity  
- Composing and authorizing the message requires screening_assigned to the originating agent, so it must remain assigned or be reassigned at authorize time.  
- Releasing screening from O after commit may conflict with authorize_settlement_message, suggesting keeping it assigned until authorization is complete.  

## Exploring Processor Readiness  
- Processors also need ready_for_processing, achieved after agents are processed, with routes allocated and other prerequisites met.  
- After message is composed and authorized, processors can stage files, assuming no exceptions and following agent completion.

## Managing Processor Resources  
- After closing settlement_processor_primary_instance (P1), it’s released, but resources like screening, configuration, escalation, and funding remain bound.  
- Release actions are needed: use release_screening_service_from_instruction, release_configuration_parameter_from_processor, release_escalation_policy_from_processor, and release_funding_source_from_instruction to free resources for P2.  

## Allocating Routes for Secondary Processor  
- For settlement_processor_secondary_instance (P2), a route is needed, but all three routes (primary, secondary, fallback) are currently allocated to O, B, and P1.  
- To allocate a route to P2, one existing instruction must be completed first to free a route, as routes are released only upon complete_settlement.

## Completing Processor Settlements  
- To allocate a route for P2, attach a duplicate (dup) to P2 and complete its settlement using the fallback route, freeing the route for use.  
- After completing P2, both processors (P1 and P2) are done, and the duplicate becomes available for further use.  

## Finalizing Agent Settlements  
- Agents (O and B) remain released but not completed, with routes still bound (O to primary, B to secondary).  
- Attach duplicates to O and B, assign screening, then complete their settlements, freeing routes and duplicates for reuse.  

## Verifying Prerequisites and Conditions  
- All prerequisites for actions like reserve_channel and authorize_message are met, with screening assigned where required.  
- No issues found with external references or service parameters, as they weren’t needed, and fallback routes were used sequentially without conflict.  

## Checking Settlement File Availability  
- Staging for P2 uses a different settlement file (secondary_instance), available after primary staging, avoiding conflicts.  
- Composing and authorizing messages satisfies staging requirements without needing a settlement_message_available flag.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
